title: Cycler Finder Catalogue
description: Schema v4.9 (task #453): adds a fifth orbit_class enum value resonant_po — a stable resonant / libration periodic orbit that is reachable at any epoch and repeats indefinitely (so it shares the cycler invariant epoch_locked=false / n_returns='infinite') but has NO demonstrated transport utility (it never encounters the secondary; see Region B #447). Distinct from cycler, whose defining property IS transport between encounters. Added so the catalogue can carry a known-class-member resonant PO (em-cycler-21-3d-spatial-2026, the 3D spatial extension of the planar (2,1) Earth-Moon cycler) without mislabelling it a transport cycler. The resonant_po-is-not-epoch-locked invariant is pinned by tests/data/test_schema_v47_orbit_class.py alongside cycler. Schema v4.8 (task #417): adds two additive optional, nullable row properties encoding the Axis-B real-ephemeris deterministic maintenance-ΔV band taxonomy from docs/notes/2026-06-22-dv-band-definitions.md — dv_band (enum strictly_ballistic/essentially_ballistic/low_maintenance/powered_dsm/low_thrust_sep or null) and its mandatory companion dv_band_source (the published source the band traces to, string or null). Orthogonal to trajectory_regime (Axis-A geometric label): dv_band is the magnitude axis, trajectory_regime the geometric axis. Both default absent/null; a band is assigned ONLY when the row already carries a sourced deterministic maintenance ΔV with a known basis (Russell-Ocampo 2006's <1/<10/<300 m/s-per-7-cycle tiers), never inferred from regime/V∞. The dv_band_source-required-when-dv_band-non-null honesty gate is enforced by the Python semantic gate. Schema v4.7 for data/catalogue.yaml. Task #294: catalogue scope expansion from cyclers-only to a four-class taxonomy (cycler / quasi_cycler / precursor_mga / mga_tour). Adds three additive optional top-level row fields — orbit_class (enum, default cycler for backward compat), epoch_locked (bool, default false), n_returns (integer or the string 'infinite', default 'infinite' for cyclers) — plus three optional epoch-locked fields validity_window {start,end}, launch_epoch, and inserts_into (cross-row pointer used by precursor_mga). The class invariants — cycler MUST be epoch_locked=false / n_returns='infinite'; quasi_cycler / precursor_mga / mga_tour MUST be epoch_locked=true with a finite integer n_returns; precursor_mga MUST resolve inserts_into to an existing cycler row — are enforced by the Python semantic gate (the JSON Schema cannot express cross-row referential integrity). Existing rows default to orbit_class='cycler' / epoch_locked=false / n_returns='infinite' under a one-time migration (scripts/migrate_catalogue_scope_2026-06-15.py), preserving comments and formatting via ruamel.yaml. Supersedes the cyclers-only scope memory (project_catalogue_scope_cyclers_only -> project_catalogue_scope_expanded_2026-06-15). Builds on v4.6, which hardens the previously-freeform discriminator string fields with controlled-vocabulary enums — model_assumption, trajectory_regime, sense, and data_gaps[].kind — each enum being the exact superset of live values plus obviously-intended members. Discovered during #184: model_assumption='bicircular' and data_gaps[].kind='not-applicable' were accepted with zero validation, so a typo (cr3pb, unkown) would pass silently and corrupt the catalogue. Builds on v4.5, which adds the additive optional top-level validation_level enum (V0-V5, the spec §14 gauntlet level; spec §16.7.12), back-filled mechanically by scripts/backfill_validation_level.py from recorded test evidence and guarded against over-claim by validate_validation_level. Builds on v4.4: the per-field provenance tag set orbit_source/vinf_source/orbit_fidelity/vinf_fidelity and the optional declared validation_tier (spec §16.7.11, Forge phase 0 task 3), back-filled mechanically from each row's existing source metadata. source keys are constrained to the SOURCE_REGISTRY enum and fidelity tiers to the Fidelity enum; the declared-tier-must-match-classify_validation rule is enforced by the Python semantic gate. Builds on v4.3 (supersedes/superseded_by, spec §16.7.10), v4.2 (segments[].center, segments[].tof_days_bounds, source_ephemeris, spec §16.7.9), v4.1 (free_return_arcs[] arc-type descriptors, spec §16.7.7) and v4. Permissive on legacy v1-v3 fields (additionalProperties: true throughout); enforces the v4 structural invariants: cycler_class enum, orbit_elements frame/center enums, period.basis item shape, and the conditional physical-invariant gate (multi-arc/non-keplerian must not have top-level a/e). Referential integrity of supersedes/superseded_by (targets must resolve to existing ids, no self-reference) is enforced by the Python semantic gate.

id.description: Stable human-readable slug for this cycler entry.

model_assumption.description: Schema v4.6 (task #194): controlled vocabulary for the dynamical model the row's published numbers were stated under. Enum is the exact superset of live values (circular-coplanar, analytic-ephemeris, cr3bp, bicircular) plus circular-inclined (the M-3D fidelity tier, present in the orbit_fidelity/vinf_fidelity enums) for forward consistency. Constrained because a typo (e.g. 'cr3pb', 'bicirular') would silently corrupt the fidelity derivation.

trajectory_regime.description: Schema v4.6 (task #194): controlled vocabulary for the propulsion regime of the trajectory. Enum is the live superset (ballistic, powered) plus low-thrust, the obviously-intended third member of the standard regime trichotomy. Constrained because a typo would pass silently.

sense.description: Schema v4.6 (task #194): controlled vocabulary for the direction sense of the leg. Enum is the exact live superset (outbound, inbound, n/a). Constrained because a typo would pass silently.

data_gaps.description: Schema v4.6 (task #194): the kind discriminator is now a controlled vocabulary. Permissive (additionalProperties: true) on all other gap fields; only kind is constrained.

kind.description: Schema v4.6 (task #194): controlled vocabulary for the gap discriminator. Enum is the exact live superset (unknown, derive, uncertain, not-applicable, unverified-provenance, resolved, conflict, anomaly). Constrained because a typo (e.g. 'unkown', 'not-aplicable') would pass silently and corrupt the review queue, the original #184 failure mode.

source_ephemeris.description: Schema v4.2 (spec §16.7.9): the ephemeris model the source paper states its published numbers were computed against (e.g. 'DE405', 'DE430', 'STOUR ephemeris'). Null/absent when the source does not state one. Non-empty when present (enforced by the Python semantic gate, since JSON Schema minLength cannot apply to the null branch).

orbit_source.description: Schema v4.4 (spec §16.7.11): provenance key (SOURCE_REGISTRY) for the row's orbit-element block (a/e/perihelion/aphelion). Back-filled mechanically from the row's existing source metadata. Absent/null = unknown provenance (untagged); a row left untagged classifies as UNVALIDATED.

vinf_source.description: Schema v4.4 (spec §16.7.11): provenance key (SOURCE_REGISTRY) for the row's vinf_kms_at_encounters multiset. When orbit_source and vinf_source are two DIFFERENT independent citations at the same fidelity, the row is CROSS_VALIDATED. Absent/null = unknown provenance (untagged).

orbit_fidelity.description: Schema v4.4 (spec §16.7.11): model-fidelity tier (Fidelity) the orbit-element block is stated at. Derived from model_assumption. A value at one tier must never be cross-validated against a value at another (the S1L1 cross-fidelity bug class). M-3D additively widened the enum with circular-inclined (real i/Ω, mean sma, eccentricity ignored) between circular-coplanar and analytic-ephemeris.

vinf_fidelity.description: Schema v4.4 (spec §16.7.11): model-fidelity tier (Fidelity) the vinf multiset is stated at. Derived from model_assumption. M-3D additively widened the enum with circular-inclined (real i/Ω, mean sma, eccentricity ignored) between circular-coplanar and analytic-ephemeris.

validation_tier.description: Schema v4.4 (spec §16.7.11): OPTIONAL declared validation tier. When present, the Python semantic gate (validate_provenance_tags) requires it to equal the tier classify_validation computes from the row's sources/fidelities — a row cannot over-claim cross_validated. Normally left absent: the tier is a computed classification (Task 4 ratchet), not stored data.

validation_level.description: Schema v4.5 (spec §16.7.12 / §14): the gauntlet validation level — the highest §14 gate the row has MECHANICALLY passed. Back-filled by scripts/backfill_validation_level.py applying §14 to recorded test evidence. The Python semantic gate (validate_validation_level) refuses any row declaring a level above V0 without a recorded mechanical-evidence pointer (the over-claim guard). Absent/null = untagged (treated as the V0 internal-consistency floor by downstream views).

dv_band.description: Schema v4.8 (task #417): Axis-B real-ephemeris deterministic maintenance-ΔV band, per docs/notes/2026-06-22-dv-band-definitions.md. strictly_ballistic = <1 m/s, essentially_ballistic = <10 m/s, low_maintenance = <300 m/s (Russell-Ocampo 2006 JGCD 29(2) census, total deterministic maneuver over a 7-cycle real-ephemeris propagation at the best launch window); powered_dsm = >=300 m/s impulsive (project-convention boundary = top of Russell's net); low_thrust_sep = SEP/continuous-thrust maintenance (regime, not magnitude). ORTHOGONAL to trajectory_regime (the Axis-A geometric label) — never merge or infer one from the other, and never infer a band from V∞ / v_infinity_leveraging_dv_kms (that is establishment, not maintenance). Absent/null = unclassified (the default for the many rows that carry no sourced per-row maintenance ΔV). When non-null, dv_band_source is mandatory (Python semantic gate).

dv_band_source.description: Schema v4.8 (task #417): the published source the dv_band assignment traces to (e.g. 'byrnes-longuski-aldrin-1993', 'mcconaghy-2006', 'liang-2024'). MANDATORY whenever dv_band is non-null — the honesty gate that every band magnitude traces to a sourced ΔV with a known basis (enforced by the Python semantic gate). Null/absent when dv_band is null.

flyby_altitudes_km.description: Schema v4.9 (task #427, M7): per-interior-flyby minimal-thrust periapsis ALTITUDE (km above the body surface) — the height that delivers the node's required turn ballistically (Jones Eq.2), capped at the body's sourced flyby floor (#426) where the turn over-demands. Sourced where a paper publishes per-flyby altitudes; otherwise computed by the M7 continuous-maintenance chain (flyby_altitudes_source distinguishes). Null/absent for the many rows with no reproduced trajectory (honest — never fabricated). Distinct from a single floor: each node carries its own optimal height.

node_index.description: Index of this flyby encounter within the cycler's node sequence.

body.description: One-letter flyby body code (e.g. 'E', 'M') matching the PLANETS registry.

altitude_km.description: Minimal-thrust periapsis altitude above the body surface, km. At the body floor (#426) when the flyby is bend-binding (then the node carries a maintenance ΔV); higher for gentle flybys that need less turn.

flyby_altitudes_source.description: Schema v4.9 (tasks #427/#428): provenance of flyby_altitudes_km. 'sourced' = published per-flyby altitudes (literature-stated); 'computed-m7' = computed by the M7 continuous-maintenance chain (geometry-determined minimal-thrust heights); 'derived' = SPK/Horizons-extracted per-flyby altitudes (our own reconstruction — high fidelity but not literature-sourced, so not golden-test-eligible). Sourced takes precedence. Null/absent when flyby_altitudes_km is null.

superseded_by.description: Schema v4.3 (spec §16.7.10): ids of catalogue rows that supersede this one (CCSDS NEXT_MESSAGE_ID analogue). Used when a row's premise is invalidated and one or more sibling rows now carry the attested truth. Each item must resolve to an existing row id and must not equal this row's own id; referential integrity is checked by the Python semantic gate. Null/absent for the common (non-superseded) case.

supersedes.description: Schema v4.3 (spec §16.7.10): ids of catalogue rows that this row supersedes (CCSDS PREVIOUS_MESSAGE_ID analogue). The inverse direction of superseded_by; the pair need not be declared symmetrically, but every id listed must resolve to an existing row and must not equal this row's own id (Python semantic gate). Null/absent for the common case.

cycler_class.description: Structural kind of orbit. Default single-ellipse preserves v3 behaviour.

orbit_class.description: Schema v4.7 (task #294, spec/catalogue-scope-expanded-2026-06-15): mission-actionable orbit class. cycler = strictly periodic, NOT epoch_locked, n_returns=infinite, transports between encounters (the original scope; Aldrin, Russell-Ocampo, Braik-Ross). quasi_cycler = closes-up-to-rotation, epoch_locked, 3-15 returns inside a planetary-alignment window (cyclers of opportunity). precursor_mga = non-repeating MGA chain that inserts a spacecraft INTO a steady-state cycler, epoch_locked, n_returns=1, MUST set inserts_into to an existing cycler row id. mga_tour = non-repeating multi-gravity-assist tour terminating at a destination (Galileo VEEGA, Cassini VVEJGA, Tito 2018 Mars free-return), epoch_locked, n_returns=1. resonant_po (schema v4.9, task #453) = a stable resonant / libration periodic orbit: NOT epoch_locked, n_returns=infinite (shares the cycler reachability/periodicity invariants) but with NO demonstrated transport utility — it never encounters the secondary (Region B #447). Carried for known-class corroboration, not as a usable cycler. Default cycler preserves v4.6 behaviour: every pre-v4.7 row is a cycler.

epoch_locked.description: Schema v4.7 (task #294): true when the trajectory exists only inside a specific real-ephemeris launch window (the epoch-locked classes), false for strictly periodic orbits reachable at any epoch. Default false preserves v4.6 behaviour. The test_schema_v47_orbit_class ratchet enforces orbit_class in {cycler, resonant_po} => epoch_locked=false / n_returns='infinite' and orbit_class in {quasi_cycler, precursor_mga, mga_tour} => epoch_locked=true with a finite integer n_returns.

n_returns.description: Schema v4.7 (task #294): number of cycler-style returns the trajectory completes within its validity window. The literal string 'infinite' for strictly periodic cyclers (the v4.6-and-prior default), a positive integer otherwise (1 for precursor_mga / mga_tour, 3-15 for quasi_cycler). Default 'infinite' preserves v4.6 behaviour.

validity_window.description: Schema v4.7 (task #294): real-ephemeris window during which the trajectory closes. Required (non-null) for epoch_locked classes; null/absent for cyclers (which have no window — they close at any epoch).

start.description: ISO-8601 timestamp (UTC) for the earliest epoch at which the trajectory closes within tolerance.

end.description: ISO-8601 timestamp (UTC) for the latest epoch at which the trajectory closes within tolerance.

launch_epoch.description: Schema v4.7 (task #294): ISO-8601 timestamp (UTC) for the published / reproduced launch epoch of an mga_tour or precursor_mga row. Null/absent for cyclers and for quasi_cyclers (which have a validity_window but not a single launch_epoch).

inserts_into.description: Schema v4.7 (task #294): catalogue row id of the steady-state cycler this precursor_mga inserts a spacecraft into. REQUIRED (non-null) when orbit_class='precursor_mga' (enforced by the Python semantic gate, which also verifies the target id resolves to an existing cycler row). Null/absent otherwise.

free_return_arcs.description: Schema v4.1 (spec §16.7.7): Russell's Earth-to-Earth free-return arc decomposition. Distinct from trajectory.segments (encounter legs). Each item is one Earth-Earth arc in Russell's notation. Null when no descriptor is available (data gap, not error). Only meaningful for multi-arc cyclers (no-op on single-ellipse / non-keplerian).

arc_type.description: Russell free-return arc type. g/G=generic, h/H=half-rev, f/F=full-rev.

resonance.description: Resonance ratio string (e.g. '3:2', '2:1', '1:1') for full-rev arcs. Always null for generic and half-rev.

tof_years.description: Time of flight in years for this Earth-Earth arc. Per Russell p.127: 'the first number in the parenthesis is the time of flight in years'. Null for full-rev arcs where TOF is determined by the M:N resonance condition, not listed explicitly.

raw_descriptor.description: Verbatim Russell descriptor token, e.g. 'g(1.4612,526.02,Ll)' or 'F(3:2,82.487,180.000)'. Preserved exactly so nothing is lost to interpretation. The arc_type/resonance fields are derived from this string.

a_au.description: Semi-major axis in AU. Null for multi-arc and non-keplerian.

e.description: Eccentricity. Null for multi-arc and non-keplerian.

cr3bp.description: CR3BP identity tuple for non-keplerian orbits.

invariants.description: Cycle-level identity descriptors for multi-arc orbits.

segments.description: OCM TRAJ-model encounter legs. Permissive (additionalProperties: true) on legacy keys; v4.2 only constrains the two additive optional keys center and tof_days_bounds.

center.description: Schema v4.2 (spec §16.7.9): body the segment's conic/arc is centred on. Absent => 'Sun' (heliocentric, the status quo). Free string (consistent with orbit_elements.center); no enum, to allow planet-centric (moon-tour) cycler segments (issue #76).

tof_days_bounds.description: Schema v4.2 (spec §16.7.9): published time-of-flight range [min, max] in days, for sources stating a range rather than a point. Both > 0 and min <= max (order enforced by the Python semantic gate). NOT required to contain tof_days when present — different model framings of the same leg may both be sourced (e.g. Aldrin outbound tof_days=146 circular-coplanar vs Rogers et al. 2012 STOUR range 161-172 d).
